vitals.context: Recovered, off pressors and off antibiotics, extubated and awake — preparing to leave the ICU. Afebrile and hemodynamically stable.
After comfort-measures decision and cessation of antibiotics: fevers and hypotension resolving, weaning off pressors; mental status improving (beginning to wake up).
Day ~16 in the ICU — persistent high fevers and refractory hypotension now on TWO vasopressors despite a fully negative infectious workup. Not improving.
ICU — ongoing fevers and pressor requirement; broad-spectrum antibiotics continuing; still unresponsive off sedation.
Post-TIPS: new high fevers and hypotension requiring vasopressors; somnolent. Blood cultures drawn; antibiotics broadened to vancomycin + meropenem.
ED presentation — 3 months of weight loss, new ascites and dyspnea. T 36.7 C, BP 100/60, HR 95, RR 18. Shortly after admission developed massive hematemesis → MTP, intubation, ICU.
Outpatient hepatology — documenting the ongoing weight decline (down from ~67 kg) and worsening appetite.
Outpatient baseline ~3 months before admission — weight ~67 kg (before the 20-lb decline).
